content:
The Valley of Ōtara

In the quiet valley of Ōtara, where mist curls like soft korowai around the hills, there lived a taniwha named Te Tahi-o-Te-Rā.

His home was a hidden cave beneath the shimmering waters, a place known to the people as Te Ana-o-Te Tahi.

The children of the valley loved to swim near the cave—but only when the water was clear.

For everyone knew: when the water glowed gold like koura, Te Tahi had returned, and it was time to leave the river in peace.
